mnemonics: Please Do Not Throw Sausage Pizza Away (Layers: Physical, Data Link, Network, Transport, Session, Presentation, Application)
Animals Take New Naps (Layers: Application, Transport, Network, Network Access)
I Saw Aunt Polly (Core Protocols: IP, TCP, UDP, ARP, ICMP)
keyPoints: The TCP/IP model has four layers: Application, Transport, Internet, and Network Access.
IP is responsible for logical addressing and routing (host-to-host).
TCP provides reliable, connection-oriented communication, while UDP provides faster, connectionless communication.
commonMistakes: Confusing the TCP/IP 4-layer model with the OSI 7-layer model.
Thinking TCP and IP are the same thing; IP handles routing, TCP handles reliable delivery.
Believing UDP is inherently 'insecure'; it's simply unreliable by design, not insecure.
